message:
fix(btree): defragment page on cell deletion; add debug/release-aware SSI test workloads

B-tree cursor page defragmentation (fsqlite-btree):
After deleting a cell from a leaf page, the remaining cells were left
in-place with a gap, and cell_content_offset was recalculated from the
minimum pointer. This left fragmented free space that was never reclaimed,
causing unnecessary page splits and wasted space over time.

Now perform inline defragmentation after deletion: compact all remaining
cells toward the end of the page, update their pointers, zero the freed
region, and reset fragmented_free_bytes and first_freeblock to 0. This
keeps pages maximally compact without requiring a separate defragmentation
pass, matching SQLite's approach of defragmenting during cell operations.

SSI serialization correctness test scaling (fsqlite-e2e):
The SSI workload tests used hardcoded constants that were tuned for
release builds, causing debug-mode CI runs to either timeout or fail
throughput assertions due to ~10x slower execution.

Add separate debug/release constants for:
- CI writer count (4 debug / 10 release)
- Transactions per writer (120 debug / 1000 release)
- Single-writer transactions (80 debug / 1000 release)
- Minimum throughput (80 txn/s debug / 1000 txn/s release)

Add elapsed-time budget assertions to catch runaway tests early.
All constants are overridable via environment variables
(FSQLITE_SSI_CI_WRITERS, etc.) for custom CI configurations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
